reports.text:
Oil Climbs Ahead of U.S.–Iran Talks; Goldman Raises Outlook

• 🛢️ Prices Edge Higher: Brent +0.8% to $71.9, WTI +0.9% to $67.1 ahead of key diplomatic talks.

• 📊 Goldman Upgrade: Bank lifts 4Q 2026 forecast to Brent $60, WTI $56 on tighter OECD inventories.

• 🔧 Supply Outlook: Expected gradual OPEC+ output increase, but overall supply seen slightly tighter.

• 🌍 Geopolitical Sensitivity: Crude likely to remain volatile around U.S.–Iran developments.

🟢 Impact: Mild Positive
Firm oil prices support upstream energy companies, though gains may stay capped by expected OPEC+ supply increases.

Sigma Advanced Systems : Co Bags ₹100 Cr Defence Order

• 🎯 Major Order Win: Company secures ~₹100 crore orders from Ministry of Defence & defence PSUs.

• 🚀 Strategic Programmes: Work spans SAM, anti-tank missiles, anti-radiation missiles & naval systems.

• 🛰️ Key Customers: Supplies to leading entities including BDL, HAL and DRDO establishments.

• 🏭 High-Value Electronics: Focus on mission-critical defence electronics & flight control systems.

• 📈 Capability Validation: Order reflects strong customer confidence and long qualification entry barriers.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Order strengthens defence order book visibility and reinforces Sigma’s positioning in high-margin defence electronics.

Cyient ; Co Incorporates New Wholly-Owned Subsidiary in USA

• 🌎 New Entity Formed: Cyient Inc incorporates CDS Lavender Holdings Inc. in Delaware, USA.

• 🏢 Ownership: New company is a 100% wholly owned subsidiary.

• 🛡️ Focus Area: Entity aligned to Aerospace & Defense segment.

• 💵 Investment Mode: Formed via cash share capital subscription; no acquisition cost disclosed.

• 🚀 Strategic Intent: Move aimed at enabling future business opportunities in the US market.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Long-Term)
Strengthens Cyient’s global structure in a high-growth aerospace/defense market, though near-term financial impact is limited.

IDFC First Bank Clarifies News; Flags Fraud Trigger Behind Stock Move

• 📰 News Clarification: Bank says routine government empanelments continue; denies any adverse mandate issue.

• 🚨 Fraud Identified: Recent stock movement linked to fraud disclosed on Feb 21, 2026, not empanelment concerns.

• 🧾 Action Taken: Matter escalated to board committee; police complaint filed.

• 🔍 Forensic Audit: KPMG appointed as independent agency to conduct forensic review.

• 🏦 Regular Ops Unaffected: Bank reiterates ongoing normal engagement with central & state governments.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Negative
While clarification removes mandate fears, the fraud disclosure and forensic probe may keep sentiment cautious in the near term.

Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman Launches National Monetisation Pipeline 2.0

NMP 2.0 Targets ₹16.72 Lakh Crore Asset Monetisation For FY26-30

5.8 Lakh Crore Private Investment Expected During FY26-30

NMP 2.0 Developed By NITI Aayog In Consultation With Infrastructure Ministries

Asset Monetisation Plan 2025-30 Announced In Union Budget 2025-26

Government Achieves Nearly 90% Of ₹6 Lakh Crore Target Under NMP 1.0

NMP 2.0 Sets 2.6 Times Higher Target Than First Phase

BSE To Exclude 4 Stocks From F&O Segment

HUDCO, Piramal Pharma, Tata Tech & Torrent Pharma F&O To Be Discontinued 

No New F&O Contracts From May 4, 2026
TRUMP: AS PRESIDENT, I DO NOT HAVE TO GO BACK TO CONGRESS TO GET APPROVAL OF TARIFFS
TRUMP: ANY COUNTRY THAT WANTS TO 'PLAY GAMES' WITH SUPREME COURT DECISION WILL BE MET WITH A MUCH HIGHER TARIFF, AND WORSE

Allcargo Terminals ; Co Signs MoU for Gurugram Logistics Infra

• 🤝 MoU Executed: ATL signs MoU with promoter-group entities AIPPL & TREL.

• 🏗️ Project Intent: Plan to lease land in Gurugram to develop Private Freight Terminal / Rail-connected ICD.

• 💰 Initial Deposit: Company has agreed to ₹5 crore deposit under MoU.

• 📦 Capacity Boost Aim: Proposed project expected to increase container handling volumes and support profitability.

• ⏳ Early-Stage Deal: Final terms to be decided upon definitive agreements and approvals.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Positive
Strategically positive for long-term logistics capacity, but near-term impact limited as the MoU is non-binding and early stage.

ZIM Laboratories Gets Approval to Dissolve Dubai Step-Down Subsidiary

• 🏢 Subsidiary Closure: DMCC authority approved closure of ZIM Laboratories Middle East DMCC.

• 📅 Effective Date: Dissolution effective 23 Feb 2026.

• 🔄 Earlier Board Nod: Move follows board approval in Aug 2025.

• 🌍 Overseas Rationalisation: Step seen as streamlining international structure.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Primarily a structural clean-up with limited immediate financial impact unless linked to broader strategic shifts.

JTL Industries : Co to Acquire 47.97% Stake in Powersol Metalcraft

• 🏭 Strategic Investment: JTL Industries approved acquisition of 47.97% equity stake in Powersol Metalcraft (PML).

• 💰 Deal Size: Acquisition to be completed for ₹8.10 crore in cash.

• 🔗 Associate Status: Post transaction, PML will become an associate company of JTL.

• 📊 Business Fit: PML operates in hot rolled steel sections, aligning with JTL’s steel pipe ecosystem.

• 🚀 Growth Intent: Management expects the deal to support expansion and long-term value creation.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Strategic stake acquisition strengthens JTL’s steel value chain and offers potential synergy benefits, though near-term financial impact is modest.

Anthem Biosciences: Converts Additional ₹275 Cr Loan into Equity of Subsidiary

• 🔄 Loan-to-Equity Move: Company executes first amendment to convert additional ₹275 Cr loan into equity of Neoanthem Lifesciences (WOS).

• 💰 Earlier Funding: Original financial assistance limit was ₹300 Cr, later enhanced to ₹550 Cr.

• 📊 Prior Conversion Done: ₹100 Cr already converted into equity earlier (Feb 2025).

• 🏢 Strengthening Subsidiary: Move aimed at capital restructuring and balance-sheet strengthening of the wholly owned arm.

• ✅ Approvals in Place: Board, Audit Committee and shareholders approvals already obtained.

🟢 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Positive
Internal restructuring improves subsidiary capital structure, but no immediate revenue or earnings impact for the parent.

PC Jeweller: Subsidiary Incorporates Step-Down Mining Arm in Chad

• 🌍 New Subsidiary Formed: PCJ Gems & Jewellery (WOS) incorporates PCJ Mining SARL in Republic of Chad.

• ⛏️ Mining Entry: Entity to focus on extraction of precious metal ores (mining, refining, trading).

• 💰 Initial Capital: Paid-up share capital at 1,000,000 CFA francs; business yet to commence.

• 🏢 Ownership: PCJ Gems & Jewellery holds 66% stake in the new subsidiary.

• 📄 Regulatory Note: Transaction not classified as related party; no major approvals required.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic Optionality)
Entry into precious metal mining may strengthen backward integration and long-term sourcing strategy, though near-term financial impact is limited.

Hedge Funds Dump Global Stocks at Fastest Pace Since April

• 📉 Heavy Selling: Hedge funds’ net equity sales hit fastest pace since April 2025, per Goldman Sachs.

• 🌍 Regions Hit: Selling led by North America and Europe, driven largely by short positions.

• 🏦 Financials Under Pressure: Financial stocks saw biggest disposals, indicating sector caution.

• 🛢️ Selective Buying: Funds were net buyers in energy, healthcare, and staples.

• 💰 Performance Still Strong: Hedge fund returns remain resilient in early 2026 despite volatility and AI uncertainty.

🔴 Impact: Negative (Near-term Sentiment)
Aggressive hedge fund selling signals risk-off positioning and may weigh on global equity sentiment in the short term.

Goldman Sees Q1 GDP Boost; Flags Moderate Growth Ahead

• 📈 Q1 GDP Pickup: Goldman Sachs expects 1Q growth at 3.4% annualized, aided by resumption of federal activity.

• ⚠️ Underlying Growth Softer: Core growth estimated at ~2.1% excluding shutdown effect, indicating moderate momentum.

• 🔮 2026 Outlook: Economy projected to expand ~2.6% by Q4 2026.

• 🧾 Key Drivers: Growth supported by easing tariff uncertainty and tax-reform tailwinds.

Acknit Industries : Co Inaugurates New Factory Unit in West Bengal

• 🏭 New Facility Launched: Company inaugurated new factory unit in Madhyamgram, West Bengal on Feb 22, 2026.

• 📍 Capacity Expansion: Move aimed at strengthening manufacturing footprint in the region.

• 👥 Official Presence: Inauguration completed in presence of company directors and officials.

• ⏳ Production Timeline Awaited: Company to share commercial production schedule later.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Capacity expansion signals growth intent and can support future revenue, though near-term impact depends on production ramp-up.

Unisem Agritech : Co Signs Seed Processing Job Work Pact with Seednet Agri

• 🤝 Agreement Signed: Unisem enters job work agreement with Seednet Agri Pvt Ltd for seed processing & packing.

• 🌾 Scope of Work: Covers maize, paddy, bajra and jowar seed processing and storage.

• ⏳ Contract Duration: Agreement valid for 11 months.

• 🔗 Related Party Deal: Transaction classified as related party, approved at arm’s length by Audit Committee.

• 🏭 Routine Business: Management states the deal is in ordinary course and does not impact control.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Positive
Operational tie-up supports business continuity, but limited financial visibility and related-party nature keep impact modest.

Greenply Ind ; Co Terminates JV with Alkemal; Moves to Full Control

• 🔚 JV Exit: Greenply and JV partner terminate 2014 JV agreement due to no active business.

• 🔄 Share Acquisition: Greenply Holdings (Singapore) to acquire 50% stake from partner.

• 🏢 Structure Simplified: Greenply Alkemal (Singapore) to become step-down wholly owned subsidiary.

• 🧹 Strategic Cleanup: Move aimed at streamlining overseas structure and improving control.

🟢 Impact: Positive
JV exit and full ownership improve corporate structure clarity and strategic flexibility, sentimentally positive.

Covidh Technologies ; Co Signs LOI to Acquire ISERA Biological

• 🤝 Acquisition Move: Company executes non-binding LOI with ISERA Biological for proposed 100% stake buy.

• 🧬 Strategic Expansion: Target to make ISERA a wholly owned subsidiary post completion.

• ⏳ Deal Conditional: Subject to definitive agreements, due diligence, and regulatory approvals.

• 📄 Early-Stage Step: LOI currently non-binding, except for standard clauses.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Positive
Strategically positive for growth diversification, but near-term impact limited until the deal is formally completed.

Pace Digitek : Co Arm Bags Battery Pack Order from Reliance

• 🔋 Order Win: Material subsidiary Lineage Power receives purchase order from Reliance Industries.

• 💰 Deal Size: Order worth ₹158.7 Cr (incl. GST) for supply of Li-ion battery packs.

• ⚡ Product Scope: Supply of 48V Li-ion battery packs (15S1P, 314Ah).

• 🇮🇳 Domestic Order: Contract awarded by domestic entity; no related-party involvement.

• 📅 Execution Timeline: Delivery scheduled by Aug 31, 2026.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Healthy order inflow from a marquee client boosts revenue visibility and strengthens Pace Digitek’s energy storage business.

Signpost ; Co Wins Exclusive Kolkata Streetscape Ad Rights

• 📢 Major Win: Granted exclusive outdoor advertising rights under Kolkata Streetscape Renaissance Project.

• 🏙️ Key Locations: Rights cover Park Street, Camac Street, Theatre Road, Park-O-Mat and other prime zones.

• 💰 Strong Revenue Visibility: Estimated gross ad revenue ~₹450 Cr over concession tenure.

• 📈 Long-Term Contract: Concession period 10 years (extendable by 2 years) with 5% escalation every 3 years.

• 🤝 PPP Model: Project awarded by Kolkata Municipal Corporation under PPP framework.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Long-term exclusive rights enhance revenue visibility and strengthen Signpost’s premium outdoor media presence.

Anondita Medicare Ltd. is showing strong operating momentum with improving margins and scale benefits.

H1 FY26 revenue grew 72% YoY to ₹5,410L, while PAT rose 116% to ₹1,302L. EBITDA margin expanded to 35.5% and PAT margin stood at 24%, reflecting solid operating leverage.

FY25 revenue was ₹7,699L with PAT of ₹1,642L, indicating a sharp improvement over previous years.

Capacity expansion is underway with existing 562 mn units and additional 1,360 mn units approved under ₹75 Cr capex. Trial production has already begun.

Global certifications like MDSAP and SABS strengthen export potential, with UNFPA qualification in advanced stages.
Airtel to invest Rs 20,000 crore in NBFC arm to scale digital lending

Prostarm ; Co Bags ₹13.43 Cr Solar Project Order from South Eastern Railway

•  Order Win: Prostarm receives Letter of Acceptance (LOA) from South Eastern Railway.

•  Project Scope: Supply, installation, and erection of Solar Power Plant at SRC jurisdiction (Phase 2).

•  Order Value: Contract size stands at ₹13.43 crore.

•  Execution Timeline: Project to be executed within 12 months from LOA date.

•  Long-Term Support: Includes 5-year CMC, adding service revenue visibility.

 Impact: Positive (Marginal)
Strengthens order book and renewable portfolio, though order size is relatively small.

Microfinance sector is showing signs of recovery

Asset quality trends have turned favourable across most MFIs, with Stage 2 & Stage 3 pools declining sequentially, collection efficiency normalising to ~99%+ levels for leading players. This indicates that the worst of portfolio deterioration is likely behind the sector.

Growth visibility is gradually improving. Disbursements are picking up, AUM growth has turned positive on a sequential basis for a many lenders.

Incremental slippages are moderating, provisioning intensity is expected to decline over the next 2-3 quarters. FY27 is likely to witness normalisation in credit costs, which should meaningfully support earnings recovery.

HBL Engineering Mgmt - 

Almost all our products took 10-15 years from the R&D concept to commercial success. Kavach R&D began in 2005, 20 years ago. At that time, the budget was very small. Every year it kept increasing. And it is only in 2025 that we are beginning to see results from this effort.

R&D is a long-gestation process; always remember, risk is huge, and the return payoff is huge!

🗞 BEAT THE STREET BULLETIN 🗞
Date: 23 February 2026
Telegram: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

📍 ORDER WIN

MOREPEN LABORATORIES: Bags multi-year global CDMO deal worth ~₹825 crore from an international pharma major.

WAAREE ENERGIES: Secures 500 MW solar module supply order from a leading IPP, execution slated for FY27.

OLECTRA GREENTECH: Receives LOAs for 1,085 electric buses from TGSRTC.

DEE DEVELOPMENT ENGINEERS: Wins multiple domestic and international contracts aggregating ~₹173 crore.

AXISCADES TECHNOLOGIES: Subsidiary Mistral Solutions secures ~₹25 crore defence order under HAL’s LCA Mk1A programme.

AKANKSHA POWER: Signs brand-label agreement with Schneider Electric India unit worth ₹15–20 crore annually.

MINI DIAMONDS: Bags ₹12 crore domestic lab-grown diamond order from a Mumbai-based client.

BALRAMPUR CHINI MILLS: Secures first institutional PLA order from Lucknow Cantonment Board.

DHRUV CONSULTANCY: Wins ₹2.88 crore NHAI supervision consultancy contract for NH-765 in Telangana.

📍 ACQUISITION

TIL: To acquire 60% stake in Tulip Compression Pvt Ltd for ~₹11.9 crore in cash to strengthen gas infra play.

📍 COLLABORATION

TATA COMMUNICATIONS: Partners with RailTel to build AI-ready digital infrastructure for government and enterprises.

HINDUSTAN ZINC: Signs MoU with Tripura Group to set up manufacturing unit at Rajasthan Zinc Park.

📍 LEADERSHIP CHANGES

SHIPPING CORPORATION OF INDIA LAND AND ASSETS: Govt approves re-designation of Director (Operations) to Director (Finance).

📍 NEW PROJECTS

GODREJ PROPERTIES: Signs JDA to develop 18-acre residential project in Thane with ₹7,500+ crore revenue potential.

STALLION INDIA FLUOROCHEMICALS: Gets land approval for HFO plant in Rajasthan with planned ₹200 crore capex.

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

AU SMALL FINANCE BANK: Clarifies Haryana government de-empanelment; says financial exposure limited and internal review underway.

📍 IT NEWS

QUICK HEAL TECHNOLOGIES / SASKEN TECHNOLOGIES: Sentiment under pressure after AI-driven code security launch raises disruption concerns.

INDIAN IT SECTOR: Bearish bets surge in IT futures ahead of expiry amid AI automation concerns.

📍 OTHERS

NHPC: Faces ₹227.8 crore arbitration award in Kishanganga project dispute; files counter-claim of ₹738.67 crore.

SEPC: Order book hits record ₹10,455 crore as of December 2025, boosting multi-year revenue visibility.

GARMENT MANTRA LIFESTYLE: Begins sampling recycled fabric T-shirts for export to African and UAE markets.

VALIANT COMMUNICATIONS: Completes defence evaluation of hardware-based network isolation ‘Kill Switch’ solution.

KHOOBSURAT LTD: Updates on brewery project; targets test production by April 2026.

AETHER INDUSTRIES: Commences commercial operations at Manufacturing Site 3++ from Feb 22, 2026.

JYOTI STRUCTURES: Successfully commissions 400 kV transmission line under 4.5 GW Khavda Phase-II project.

UNIHEALTH HOSPITALS: Reports first successful IVF twin birth at its Uganda facility.

URBAN COMPANY: InstaHelp crosses 50,000 daily bookings within one year of launch.

---

📍 STAY UPDATED WITH BEAT THE STREET 🚀
For more updates, join our Telegram channel: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

Trump Signals Tougher Trade Tools After Court Ruling

• 🏛️ Court Reaction: Trump says Supreme Court ruling expanded his trade powers.

• 💰 Tariff Strategy: Indicates potential use of licenses and alternative tariffs on foreign countries.

• 🌍 Trade Tensions Risk: Remarks hint at more aggressive US trade actions ahead.

• ⚠️ Policy Uncertainty: Signals possible policy volatility in global trade framework.

🔴 Impact: Negative (Global Trade Sentiment)
Rising protectionist rhetoric may increase trade tensions, creating uncertainty for global markets and exporters.

US Evacuates Lebanon Staff Amid Rising Iran Tensions

• 🚨 Evacuation Move: U.S. begins embassy staff evacuation from Lebanon as regional risks escalate.

• 🪖 Military Buildup: Washington deploys additional air and naval assets across the Middle East.

• 🔥 Iran Unrest: Protests intensify in Tehran with clashes reported at major universities.

• ⚠️ Strike Risk: Reports suggest possible limited U.S. strikes on Iranian targets under consideration.

• 🌍 High Volatility: Diplomacy continues but seen as last window to avoid wider conflict.

🔴 Impact: Negative (Global Markets)
Rising geopolitical tensions may push oil prices higher, increase volatility, and trigger risk-off sentiment in equities.

Trump May Tone Down Tariff Threats Ahead of Elections: Berenberg

• 🇺🇸 Policy Shift Likely: Analyst suggests U.S. tariff stance may soften as elections approach.

• 📊 Cost Backfire: Studies show 2025 tariffs largely hurt U.S. firms and consumers.

• 💰 Inflation Pressure: Rising domestic costs could push policymakers toward a less aggressive trade approach.

• 🗳️ Election Factor: Potential moderation linked to November midterm election dynamics.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Global Markets)
Easing tariff rhetoric could reduce trade tensions, supporting global equities, exporters, and risk sentiment.

STOCK MARKET UPDATES: Key Developments Today

BPCL Central Excise Demand  
- Commissioner of Central Tax & Central Excise, Kochi, confirms ₹1,816.65 Cr demand.  
- Breakdown: Duty ₹476.94 Cr, Interest ~₹1,339.70 Cr, Penalty ₹0.095 Cr.  
- Dispute covers Sep 2004-May 2010 transaction values under Central Excise law.  
- BPCL plans appeal before CESTAT.  
- Impact: Negative (Legal Overhang) – Sentiment pressure likely, financial hit depends on appeal.  

Rithwik Facility Management Income Tax Notice  
- Intimation from CPC, Income Tax Dept for ₹61,150 demand in AY 2025-26 due to TDS difference.  
- Company to reconcile records and take appropriate action.  
- No penalty or restriction imposed.  

NLC India Credit Rating Update  
- ICRA reaffirms '[ICRA] AAA (Stable)' on ₹2,000 Cr Long Term Non-Convertible Debentures.  
- Signals very strong safety for timely financial obligations.  
- Positive for funding access and financial standing.  

TVS Supply Chain Solutions Q3 FY26 Highlights  
- Revenue ₹2,715.8 Cr (+11.1% YoY, +2.0% QoQ).  
- Adjusted EBITDA ₹199.3 Cr (+31.2% YoY, +11.6% QoQ), margin 7.3% (vs 6.2% YoY).  
- PBT ₹16.0 Cr (turnaround from ₹-15.2 Cr YoY).  
- PAT ₹11.2 Cr (vs ₹-23.8 Cr YoY).  

Basilic Fly Studio Q3 & 9M FY26 Earnings  
- Standalone India: Revenue 2.1x YoY for 9M; Q3 ₹39 Cr, 9M ₹94 Cr (+28% vs FY25 full year).  
- EBITDA margin Q3 42.4% (vs 31.1% YoY), 9M 46.5% (vs 42.4%).  
- PAT margin Q3 27.1% (vs 10.7%), 9M 29.2% (vs 23.5%).  
- Consolidated: Q3 Revenue ₹105 Cr, 9M ₹294 Cr (1.7x YoY); Order book ₹300+ Cr (₹200+ pending).  

Sadhav Shipping Work Order  
- Secured ₹7.35 Cr contract from Chennai Port Authority for 36 mooring personnel supply.  
- Duration: 3 years (extendable by 1 year).  
- Awarded Feb 20, countersigned Feb 23, 2026; monthly payments on invoicing.  
- Bolsters order book and revenue stream.  

Unicommerce eSolutions Q3 & 9M FY26 Highlights  
- Q3 Consolidated Revenue ₹56.4 Cr (+72.2% YoY), Adj. EBITDA ₹13.4 Cr (+51% YoY).  
- 9M Revenue ₹152.7 Cr (+70.6% YoY), Adj. EBITDA ₹34.3 Cr (+75.8% YoY).  
- Annualized Revenue Run Rate >₹225 Cr, Adj. EBITDA >₹53 Cr.  
- Q3 PAT ₹7.4 Cr (excl. non-cash amortization).  

Taj GVK Hotels VAT Demands  
- Notices from Assistant Commissioner (ST), Khairatabad for FY 2008-09 post tribunal dismissal.  
- Taj Krishna: Balance payable ₹6,67,849 (after ₹6,67,849 deposited).  
- Taj Deccan: Balance payable ₹2,44,146 (after ₹2,44,146 deposited).  

EU-US Trade Tariff Risk  
- EU Commissioner Sečević to discuss tariffs with G7 trade ministers.  
- EU may freeze US trade agreement approvals due to Trump-era tariff risks.  
- Impact: Neutral to Slightly Negative – Short-term uncertainty for exporters.  

Colgate Haryana Oral Health Program  
- Partners with Haryana Govt under Bright Smiles, Bright Futures for 57 lakh school children.  
- Covers public/private schools with preventive education.  
- Enhances brand equity and long-term consumer connect.  
- Impact: Positive (Sentimentally), modest financial effect.  

Talbros Auto JV Approval  
- MCA approves Lohum Talbros CarbonTech Pvt Ltd incorporation.  
- JV with Lohum CleanTech for advanced carbon/clean-tech entry.  
- Approved Feb 22, 2026; strengthens EV/clean mobility presence.  
- Impact: Positive.  

TCC Concept NSE Listing  
- NSE approves listing/trading of 4.75 Cr equity shares under symbol “TCC”.  
- Effective Feb 25, 2026.  
- Boosts liquidity and investor participation.  
- Impact: Positive.  

Aequs 50% Stake in Foundation  
- Board approves secondary acquisition of 1,000 shares (50%) in Aequs Foundation for ₹10,000.  
- Related party, arm’s length; improves CSR control/visibility.  
- Completion within one month.  
- Impact: Neutral.  

Tiger Logistics Gujarat Expansion  
- Strengthens Gujarat ops amid solar EXIM boom.  
- Tigreen handles ~2,000 TEUs/month, Gujarat bulk.  
- Solar volumes +40-45% YoY expected.  
- Appoints GM-Gujarat for execution.  
- Impact: Positive for volume growth.

Dhruv Consultancy NHAI Order  
- ₹2.88 Cr (ex-GST) Supervision Consultancy for 85.8 km NH-765 in Telangana.  
- 36-month execution from Hyderabad NHAI.  
- No related party involvement.  
- Impact: Positive order book addition.  

Waaree Energies 500 MW Order  
- One-time supply of 500 MW solar modules to domestic IPP/developer.  
- For FY2026-27 delivery.  
- No promoter interest; reinforces market position.  

Key Takeaway  
Mixed bag with robust earnings/orders in renewables/logistics outweighing tax overhangs – track appeals and execution.

Must Visit ⤵️
@Fundamental3

Yash Innoventures – ₹120 Cr Real Estate Project Update  

Project Expansion:  
-  Company initiating a real estate development project valued at around ₹120 crore.  

Land Aggregation:  
-  Successfully completed acquisition of 24 residential flats as part of land assembly process.  

Key Approval:  
-  Obtained Non-Agricultural (NA) permission for the identified project land.  

Growth Milestone:  
-  Marks company’s entry into the real estate development segment, diversifying its business portfolio.  

Impact:  
-  Positive at an early stage – enhances long-term growth visibility, though execution and monetisation remain key factors to track.  

Key Takeaway:  
Yash Innoventures’ foray into real estate signals strategic expansion with promising potential ahead.

TBO Tek ; Co Appoints Aditi Madhok-Naarden as Global CHRO

• 👩‍💼 Senior Hire: Ms. Aditi Madhok-Naarden joins as Global Chief Human Resources Officer effective Feb 23, 2026.

• 🌍 Global Experience: Brings 20+ years of international HR leadership across tech, BFSI, FMCG and global firms.

• 🚀 People Strategy Focus: Will lead TBO’s global people strategy as the company scales internationally.

• 🧠 Leadership Strengthening: Appointment aimed at building high-performance, diverse and scalable workforce.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Senior global HR leadership strengthens management bandwidth and supports TBO’s global expansion strategy.

SCI ; Govt Approves Post Re-Designation of Director Role

• 🏛️ Administrative Update: Ministry of Ports, Shipping & Waterways (MoPSW) informed approval from DPE.

• 🔄 Role Change: Existing Director (Operations) post re-designated as Director (Finance) at SCILAL.

• 📅 Effective Date: Communication received on 23 Feb 2026.

• 📌 Nature of Event: Purely organizational/administrative restructuring.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Leadership re-designation has no immediate financial or operational impact on the company.

Waaree Energies ; Co Bags 500 MW Solar Module Supply Order

• ☀️ Order Win: Company receives order to supply 500 MW solar modules from a leading IPP customer.

• 🏭 Customer Profile: Order from a renowned solar power developer (IPP).

• 📅 Execution Timeline: Supply scheduled during FY27.

• 🔄 Order Nature: One-time supply contract in the normal course of business.

• 🔒 Governance Comfort: No promoter or related-party involvement.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Large module order strengthens order visibility and reinforces Waaree’s position in the solar supply chain.

Dhruv Consultancy ; Co Wins NHAI Supervision Contract

• 🛣️ LOA Received: Company secures Supervision Consultancy (SC) order from NHAI (Hyderabad).

• 📍 Project Scope: O&M supervision for 85.8 km stretch of NH-765 in Telangana (PMC mode).

• 💰 Order Value: Contract worth ₹2.88 Cr (ex-GST).

• ⏳ Execution Timeline: Project to be completed over 36 months.

• 🔒 No Related Party: Order awarded in normal course; no promoter interest involved.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Order win strengthens order book visibility, though ticket size is relatively small.
EU Commission Spokesperson: Commissioner Sečević is to meet with G7 trade ministers on Monday afternoon to discuss tariffs

Aequs : Co to Acquire 50% Stake in Aequs Foundation

• 🤝 Strategic Stake Buy: Board approves acquisition of 1,000 equity shares (50%) in Aequs Foundation via secondary deal.

• 🧾 Related Party Deal: Transaction classified as related-party but executed on arm’s length basis.

• 🎯 Purpose: Move aimed at improving visibility and control over CSR initiatives of the group.

• 💰 Deal Size: Total consideration of ₹10,000 (cash) — financially immaterial.

• ⏳ Timeline: Acquisition expected to be completed within one month of approval.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Transaction is small and CSR-focused; limited direct financial impact on Aequs’ core business.

Tiger Logistics Expands Western India Presence Amid Solar EXIM Boom

• 📍 Strategic Expansion: Strengthens Gujarat presence, a key solar EXIM hub.

• 🚚 Volume Growth Visibility: Tigreen vertical handles ~2,000 TEUs/month, with Gujarat contributing the majority.

• 📈 Strong Outlook: Solar container volumes expected to grow 40–45% YoY.

• 👔 Leadership Strengthening: Appoints General Manager – Gujarat to boost execution and customer reach.

• ☀️ Sector Tailwinds: Expansion aligned with India’s rising solar manufacturing and export momentum.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Positions Tiger Logistics to capture rising solar EXIM demand and supports medium-term volume growth visibility.

COLGATE ; Colgate Palmolive Partners Haryana Govt for Oral Health Program

• 🤝 Strategic Tie-up: Partners with Haryana Government under Bright Smiles, Bright Futures initiative.

• 🎯 Large Outreach: Program to cover 57 lakh school children across the state.

• 📚 Preventive Focus: Aims to embed oral health education in public & private school systems.

• 🌍 Strong Brand Visibility: Expands Colgate’s flagship social initiative footprint in India.

• 📈 Long-Term Engagement: Supports sustained consumer connect and brand recall among young users.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Sentimentally)
Strengthens brand equity and long-term market presence, though immediate financial impact is limited.

BHARTI AIRTEL: Bharti Airtel Plans Big NBFC Expansion via Airtel Money

• 💰 Large Capital Infusion: Airtel to invest ₹20,000 Cr to scale its NBFC platform.

• 🏦 RBI License Secured: Airtel Money received NBFC registration on Feb 13, 2026.

• 📊 Strong Early Traction: Lending platform already crossed ₹9,000 Cr disbursements with healthy asset quality.

• 📱 Digital Advantage: To leverage Airtel’s large customer base, data analytics and LSP model.

• 🚀 New Growth Engine: Move aimed at deepening financial inclusion and diversifying revenue streams.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Aggressive NBFC scale-up can unlock a new high-growth fintech vertical for Airtel and improve long-term earnings visibility.

Talbros Auto Gets MCA Nod for JV with Lohum CleanTech

• 🤝 JV Progress: MCA approves incorporation of JV Lohum Talbros CarbonTech Pvt Ltd.

• 🔋 Strategic Move: JV formed with Lohum CleanTech to enter advanced carbon/cleantech space.

• 📜 Regulatory Milestone: Approval received on Feb 22, 2026, advancing earlier board decision.

• 🚀 Future Opportunity: Expected to strengthen presence in EV/clean mobility value chain.

🟢 Impact: Positive
JV approval opens new growth avenue in the emerging clean-tech and battery ecosystem.

TCC Concept Gets NSE Listing & Trading Approval

• 📜 NSE Approval: Company receives listing and trading approval from NSE.

• 🧾 Shares Listed: 4.75 crore equity shares approved for trading.

• 🏷️ Trading Symbol: Will trade on NSE under the symbol “TCC”.

• 📅 Effective Date: Listing to be effective from February 25, 2026.

• 📈 Liquidity Boost: Dual listing expected to improve stock visibility and investor participation.

🟢 Impact: Positive
NSE listing enhances liquidity, market reach, and institutional visibility for the stock.

Fractal : Co Launches ‘PiEvolve’ Agentic AI Engine; Achieves Top ML-Bench Ranking

• 🤖 New AI Launch: Fractal unveils PiEvolve, an agentic engine for autonomous ML and scientific discovery.

• 🏆 Benchmark Milestone: Ranked among top-performing agents on OpenAI’s ML-Bench, crossing key performance thresholds.

• ⚡ Advanced Capability: Designed for continuous self-improvement, reasoning, code generation, and validation loops.

• 🌐 Enterprise Focus: Targets complex use cases across supply chains, financial services, and data-driven operations.

• 📈 Innovation Push: Strengthens Fractal’s positioning in the fast-growing enterprise AI and agentic AI space.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic)
Enhances technology leadership and long-term growth narrative, though near-term financial impact may be limited.

Ashish Dhawan holds stocks worth of Rs. 1,987

Top 5 stocks account for 80% of Portfolio

Yash Innoventures : Co Announces ₹120 Cr Real Estate Project Update

• 🏗️ Project Expansion: Company undertaking real estate development project of ~₹120 crore.

• 🏠 Land Aggregation Progress: Completed acquisition of 24 residential flats as part of land assembly.

• 📜 Key Approval Received: Obtained Non-Agricultural (NA) permission for the project land.

• 📊 Growth Milestone: Move marks company’s expansion into real estate development segment.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Early Stage)
Strategically positive for growth visibility, but execution and monetisation remain key monitorables.

Prostarm ; Co Bags ₹13.43 Cr Solar Project Order from South Eastern Railway

• 🏆 Order Win: Prostarm receives Letter of Acceptance (LOA) from South Eastern Railway.

• ☀️ Project Scope: Supply, installation, and erection of Solar Power Plant at SRC jurisdiction (Phase 2).

• 💰 Order Value: Contract size stands at ₹13.43 crore.

• ⏳ Execution Timeline: Project to be executed within 12 months from LOA date.

• 🔧 Long-Term Support: Includes 5-year CMC, adding service revenue visibility.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Marginal)
Strengthens order book and renewable portfolio, though order size is relatively small.

Patel Engineering JV Wins ₹133 Cr Irrigation Project as L1 Bidder

• 🏆 L1 Win: Patel Engineering (with JV partner) declared lowest bidder for ₹133.25 Cr irrigation project from MKVDC.

• 💰 Company Share: Patel Engineering’s share in the contract stands at ₹67.96 Cr.

• 🚧 Project Scope: Includes construction of head works, pump houses, pipelines, and allied civil & electro-mechanical works.

• ⏳ Execution Timeline: Project to be completed within 48 months.

• 🌾 Irrigation Boost: Project to serve ~2,277 hectares irrigation command area in Satara district, Maharashtra.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Order win strengthens order book visibility, though size is relatively moderate.

HFCL : HFCL Joins IIT Delhi–Led Consortium for Hollow-Core Fiber Tech

• 🤝 Strategic Collaboration: HFCL joins DoT-funded consortium led by IIT Delhi to develop next-gen hollow-core fiber (HCF).

• ⚡ Advanced Technology: HCF expected to reduce latency and energy consumption vs conventional optical fiber.

• 🌐 Future Networks Focus: Technology seen as critical for 6G, AI data centers, quantum networks and ultra-low-latency infra.

• 🏭 Capability Boost: HFCL to contribute manufacturing and application expertise for commercial deployment pathway.

• 🇮🇳 Indigenous Push: Initiative aims to strengthen India’s self-reliance in advanced optical communication tech.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic)
Enhances HFCL’s positioning in next-gen telecom technologies, though financial gains likely medium-to-long term.

Transindia Real Estate ; Co Signs MoU for Rail-Connected ICD Project

• 🤝 Strategic MoU: Transindia Real Estate (TREL) signs MoU with Allcargo Inland Park (AIPPL) and Allcargo Terminals (ATL).

• 🚆 Logistics Infra Plan: Proposal to develop and operate a Private Freight Terminal / Rail-Connected ICD at Gurugram, Haryana.

• 🏗️ Asset Monetisation: AIPPL’s land to be leveraged for logistics infrastructure development.

• 💰 Recurring Income Potential: Arrangement expected to generate stable lease income with periodic escalation.

• 📊 Early-Stage Deal: MoU is non-binding; detailed agreements subject to approvals.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Long Term)
Strengthens logistics ecosystem and land monetisation visibility, though near-term impact limited until final execution.

Market Closes In Green; IT Weak, Midcaps Lag

• 📈 Benchmark Gains: Sensex up 480 pts to 83,295, Nifty up 142 pts to 25,713.

• 🟢 Overall Positive: Broader market closed largely in the green with Nifty above 25,700.

• 📉 Midcap Pressure: Midcap index fell 258 pts to 59,256, showing underperformance.

• 🏦 Bank Support: Nifty Bank rose 92 pts to 61,264, lending support to markets.

• 💻 IT Weakness: Nifty IT declined for the third straight session.

• 🔻 Top Losers: IDFC First Bank, UPL, AU SFB among key laggards.

Anondita Medicare Ltd. is showing strong operating momentum with improving margins and scale benefits.

H1 FY26 revenue grew 72% YoY to ₹5,410L, while PAT rose 116% to ₹1,302L. EBITDA margin expanded to 35.5% and PAT margin stood at 24%, reflecting solid operating leverage.

FY25 revenue was ₹7,699L with PAT of ₹1,642L, indicating a sharp improvement over previous years.

Capacity expansion is underway with existing 562 mn units and additional 1,360 mn units approved under ₹75 Cr capex. Trial production has already begun.

Global certifications like MDSAP and SABS strengthen export potential, with UNFPA qualification in advanced stages.

reports.text:
Marathon Nextgen Q3 & 9M FY26 Earnings Highlights

Financial Performance  
- Highest ever PAT ₹161 Cr.  
- Driven by strong commercial portfolio + steady residential.  

9M FY26 Metrics  
- Total revenue ₹487 Cr.  
- Area sales: 1.8 lakh sq ft (pre-merger), 2.46 lakh sq ft (post-merger).  
- Booking value: ₹421 Cr (pre-merger), ₹628 Cr (post-merger).  
- Collections: ₹578 Cr (pre-merger), ₹798 Cr (post-merger).  
- Net debt-free balance sheet.  

Key Takeaway  
Record ₹161 Cr PAT + debt-free status underscore Marathon Nextgen's robust execution.
Share India Securities Limited 🚀 has approved an additional investment of ₹5.99 Crore (₹5,99,39,940) in its subsidiary, Share India Cred Capital Private Limited (SICCPL).

eMudhra Surge: PQC Readiness Demand

Quantum Risk Rise  
- Surge in global enterprise demand for Post-Quantum Cryptography advice.  
- Transition strategies needed as quantum computing advances.  

Threat Escalation  
- Legacy crypto algorithms at risk.  

Regulatory Push  
- Standards bodies urging quantum threat assessments.  
- Focus on long-lived data & digital identities.  

Boardroom Priority  
- Executives seek clarity on PQC timelines, risks, migration paths.  

Key Takeaway  
Quantum threat drives eMudhra's PQC consulting boom.

• 🔐 Strategic Focus: eMudhra sees post-quantum cryptography (PQC) becoming a board-level priority globally.

• 🌍 Rising Engagement: Company reports increased demand from enterprises & institutions for quantum-risk readiness.

Disruption Risk  
- AI poses structural threat to broader IT/ITES.

- SaaS players hold strong data moats.

- AI simplifies cycles.  
- Potential spend reallocation from maintenance to innovation if costs fall.  

Key Takeaway  
AI compresses IT/ITES margins/contracts short-term, but unlocks innovation upside long-term.

India-France DTAC Protocol Amended

Agreement Signed  
- Protocol amends Double Taxation Avoidance Convention (DTAC).  

Dividend Tax Changes  
- Modifies taxation framework for dividend income.  
- Replaces single 10% tax rate.  

New Split Structure  
- 5% tax for shareholders with ≥10% equity capital.  
- 15% tax for all other cases.  

Strategic Impact  
- Lower 5% rate boosts investment attractiveness.  
- Supports cross-border capital flows.  

Key Takeaway  
5% dividend tax for 10%+ holders makes India-France investments more appealing.

IDFC First Bank Chandigarh-Haryana Fraud Concall

Fraud Nature  
- Limited to one Chandigarh branch, Haryana govt-linked accounts.  
- Modus: Forged cheques/debit instructions processed via employee-external collusion.  
- Classic physical cheque fraud; bypassed maker-checker/AML controls.  
- No senior mgmt involvement; implicated staff suspended.  

Quantum & Mitigants  
- Headline impact ₹590 Cr (₹490 Cr discrepancy + ₹100 Cr suspect accounts).  
- Conservative estimate; minor adjustments expected.  
- Recovery: Lien/blocks on traced funds, ₹35 Cr insurance.  
- Legal sharing pending forensic/legal process.  
- Q4 P&L hit via provisions; still profitable, above ₹500 Cr PAT run-rate.  

Deposit Risks  
- Haryana deposits 0.5% total; ₹200 Cr outflow manageable.  
- Govt/PSU deposits 8-10% (industry norm).  
- Deep govt integrations (GST/CBDT) to limit contagion.  
- Some de-panelments expected; focus on responsibility acceptance.  

Controls Failure & Fixes  
- Existing: Maker-checker, ₹5L/10L verifications, AML/mule alerts.  
- Failed due to collusion on physical cheques.  
- Enhancements: Mandatory app alerts for high-value, AI signature verification, staff monitoring, govt balance reconciliation.  

Forensic & Timeline  
- KPMG appointed; 4-5 weeks for report.  
- Police complaints filed, liens initiated, board/RBI informed.  

Mgmt Tone & Guidance  
- First major incident in 10 years; "eye-opener".  
- Core metrics strong: Op profit margin >2%, NIM 5.7-5.8%, falling credit costs.  
- Well-capitalised (₹46,000 Cr NW); Q4 growth intact.  
- Own faults, pursue others; "look through" to profitability trajectory.  

Key Takeaway  
₹590 Cr ring-fenced fraud provisionable in Q4 without derailing franchise or growth.

India Export Relief: US Tariff Drops to 15%

Tariff Backstory  
- India faced 50% US tariff (25% reciprocal + 25% for Russian oil).  
- India-US interim trade deal this month cut it to 18%.  
- Supreme Court invalidated Trump's tariff orders entirely.  
- Trump invoked Section 122 Trade Act for flat 15% global tariff on all imports, effective Feb 26.  

Impact for Indian Exporters  
- Down from 50% to 15% - massive improvement.  
- ~55% of India's US-bound exports freed from 18% reciprocal duty.  
- Revert to standard MFN rates.  

Benefiting Sectors  
- Textiles.  
- Marine products (shrimp).  
- Pharma.  
- Gems.  

Key Takeaway  
15% flat tariff + MFN reversion breathes life into textiles, gems, pharma exporters.

RBI Norms to Hit Banks' Fee Income

Key Impacts  
- Draft guidelines target mis-selling of financial products.  
- Tighter broker funding norms expected to dent non-interest income.  

Bancassurance Exposure FY25  
- HDFC Bank: ₹6,308 Cr (13.8% other income).  
- Axis Bank: ₹3,174 Cr (12.5%).  
- SBI: ₹2,766.8 Cr (4.5%).  
- Third-party products: 24.7% total fee income (up from 22% FY24).  

Mis-Selling Rules  
- Prohibit bundling third-party products with core offerings.  
- Require explicit customer consent.  
- Ensure product suitability, transparent disclosures.  
- Mandate compensation for mis-selling.  

Broker Funding Tightening  
- No funding brokers for proprietary trades.  
- Back exposures with 100% collateral (significant cash component).  
- Broker guarantees need 50% collateral, min 25% cash.  

Timeline & Effects  
- Effective from July; final framework in February.  
- May reduce float income, commissions; hits large private banks harder.  

Key Takeaway  
RBI curbs on mis-selling & broker funding to squeeze bancassurance/commission streams for banks.

- SWAMIH housing fund: ₹1,073 Cr  

Strategic Impact  
- Strong CPSE profitability supports fiscal stability, non-tax revenue growth.  

Key Takeaway  
CPSE dividends on pace to beat target, bolstering Centre's FY26 fiscal position.

**Exposure, deposits and quantification
**• Haryana-related deposits constitute ~0.5% of total bank deposits.
• The observed outflow from these accounts is approximately INR 2bn.
• The total exposure is estimated at ~INR 5.9bn, comprising:
o INR 4.9bn identified initially, and
o An additional ~INR 1bn identified subsequently.
• Management has clearly communicated the outer limit of the incident without delay.
• Government deposits (state + central) typically account for 8–10% of overall deposits.
 
**Detection, background and timeline**
• The bank was first notified by the Haryana State Government, with the issue gaining momentum post 18 February.
• One customer highlighted discrepancies in account statements, after which the bank reconciled other related accounts.
• There was no error in system-generated statements; alerts, SMS notifications and transaction statements were duly sent.
• No communication has been received from any other state government regarding similar issues.
 
**Nature of the incident
**• The incident is limited to a single branch and involved multiple accounts.
• This was not a system failure but a case of employee collusion with third parties.
• The transactions were executed through the cheque-based system, not electronic channels.
• The bank has stated this is a one-off incident, given its otherwise strong operating track record.
• The incident has deeply disturbed management, and decisive steps will be taken to address collusion-related risks.
 
**Recovery actions and Inter-bank coordination**
• The bank is analyzing the incident across Level 1, 2 and 3 scenarios to identify funds not legitimately disbursed.
• Recovery efforts are underway, including from other linked accounts.
• Funds were transferred to multiple other banks, all of other banks which have been cooperative.
• Certain recipient accounts required additional confirmations as part of the recovery process.
• The bank expects further recoveries from the identified accounts.
 
**Controls, checks and preventive framework
**• Transactions above INR 0.5mn are covered under a Positive Pay system, with customer cross-verification.
• Transactions above INR 1mn undergo additional verification layers.
• The bank has introduced a monthly balance monitoring system as part of fraud prevention.
• Going forward, the bank will introduce digitally verified stipulations for transaction authorisation.
• Enhanced measures are being implemented specifically to prevent collusion risks.
• Government accounts continue to receive heightened sensitivity and oversight.
** 
Forensic audit and accountability**
• A forensic audit has been initiated, covering the entire chain of events.
• KPMG has been appointed, with the process expected to take 4–5 weeks.
• The forensic review will verify facts, determine responsibility and assess intent, with a focus on fairness.
• The bank is engaging with employees and counterparties involved and will proceed strictly as per the law.
 
**Financial Impact, Insurance and capital
**• Bank holds an employee dishonesty insurance policy of approximately INR 350mn.
• The bank’s LCR stands at ~115%, well above regulatory requirements.
• Capital adequacy remains comfortable and in excess of minimum norms.
• Core operations remain strong, though the bank may take appropriate provisions.
 
**Business outlook and management confidence
**• Bank maintains a strong reputation in the market, despite recent interest rate reductions.
• Prior to the incident, the bank was expecting a strong quarter, supported by better NIMs and lower credit costs.
• Management reiterated its confidence in the strength of core operations and long-term franchise stability.

**IDFC FIRST BANK CONCALL ON FRAUD OF INR5.9B
** 
**CONCALL KTA
Remarks by management
**• The matter is restricted to the Chandigarh branch, with no involvement of any other branches.
• Employees from this branch were involved and have since been suspended.
• The bank has appointed KPMG to conduct an audit.
• Police complaints have already been filed in connection with the incident.
• Recovery efforts have commenced, and existing control mechanisms remain in place.
• The identified discrepancy stands at INR 4.9bn, with the bank conservatively recognising an additional INR 1bn, taking the total estimated impact to INR 5.9bn. This figure is considered broadly accurate at present.
• The bank remains well-capitalized, with profitability continuing on a positive trajectory.
• This was an isolated branch-level group incident, where debit instructions originated from the client and funds were transferred to external parties, with involvement of branch employees.
• The incident occurred with the connivance of certain employees, and the bank has moved swiftly by initiating a forensic audit.
• Necessary corrective actions will be taken to contain the issue and prevent any recurrence.
• The Board has taken cognisance of the matter and is acting in line with regulatory guidance.
• The bank operates over 1,000 branches and has not encountered a similar fraud in the past.
• The bank expects NIMs of approximately 5.8% for the current quarter.

🚀 GANGA FORGING – Q3FY25 RECOVERY WITH RAILWAY EXPANSION

Ganga Forging is showing operational stabilisation while entering a high entry barrier railway segment.

📊 Q3FY25 – Sequential Improvement

• Revenue grew 7% QoQ to ₹887.6 Lkhs
• EBITDA at ₹40.1 Lkhs, margin ~4.5%
• Net profit positive at ₹3.94 Lkhs
• Lower finance cost supporting stability

🚆 Elastic Rail Clip Entry

• Awaiting RDSO approval
• Capacity: 3.25 lakh clips per month
• Eligible to supply across railway zones post approval

💰 Upside Potential

• Realisation ~₹120 per clip
• ~₹3.9 Cr monthly potential
• ~₹48 Cr annual opportunity
• ~20% expected net margin

🎯 Why It Matters

• High entry barrier segment
• Margin accretive mix
• Strong operating leverage potential

Overall View: Recovery plus railway entry strengthens growth outlook for Ganga Forging.

India's data centers require sophisticated power infrastructure, accounting for nearly 10% of total capital costs, with advanced setups using 2N or N+1 redundancies, modular power/thermal management, and intelligent PDUs for real-time PUE monitoring.

Power Flow Chart
Key Equipment Stages
- Utility to MV: High-voltage step-down via MV transformers and switchgear.
- Redundancy Layer: STS/ATS for seamless failover.
- LV Distribution: Transformers to PDUs feeding IT racks.

Major components include MV transformers, switchgear, protection gear, transfer switches, and PDUs, ensuring reliable power delivery to critical IT loads.

SEPC Order Book Hits Record ₹10,455 Crore

• 🌍 Balanced Geography: Domestic orders ~48%; international portfolio adds diversification.

• 📈 Revenue Visibility: Strong inflows and execution pipeline enhance multi-year growth visibility.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Record order book and strong inflows improve revenue visibility and strengthen SEPC’s growth outlook.

US tariff policy is now a moving target and Indian exporters are paying the price.

In just months, duties swung from MFN-only to +50%, then back to +25%, with fresh Section 122 tariffs layered in. SCOTUS  rulings, Russia oil-linked penalties, and sudden revisions have turned trade into a guessing game.

Exporters say policy volatility is the real disruptor. Discounts to retain US buyers have ranged from 0% to 18%, with fears of demands going up to 50% if uncertainty persists.

Footwear, garments and leather players see some short-term relief as duties settle in the 10–15% range. But the bigger risk? China may emerge the biggest beneficiary if instability continues.

*Nomura: IDFC First Bank | Fraud disclosure to weigh on near-term sentiment*

*What really happened?*

IDFC First Bank reported potential fraud at its Chandigarh branch involving certain employees and specific Haryana government-linked accounts. Discrepancies were identified in account balances following a closure and fund transfer request. The aggregate amount under reconciliation is INR5.9bn, with the final impact subject to validation, recoveries and legal proceedings. The issue is stated to be confined to identified government accounts and not other branch customers. The bank has suspended four officials, informed regulators and auditors, filed police complaints, initiated lien marking on beneficiary accounts, and appointed an independent forensic auditor.

*Potential impact on financials*

The amount under reconciliation (INR5.9bn) forms 28% of FY26F profit and 19bp of the CET-1 ratio, which stood at 14.23% as of Dec-25.

The exact impact to the bank’s﻿ financials will depend on potential recoveries made through the liens marked on fraudulent beneficiary accounts maintained with other banks, liabilities of entities involved in the transactions and the legal recovery process.

*Our view*

While the issue appears localized, it raises concerns around governance and branch-level controls. We await clarity on the quantum of potential loss, recoveries, and provisioning stance.

In deposit-linked frauds, banks usually protect depositors and recognize the loss through P&L once the fraud is established, leading to high/often full provisioning, whereas recoveries, if any, are generally back-ended (insurance, asset seizure, third-party liability).

Given IDFC First Bank’s retail deposit-led model, reputational perception remains critical, and the stock could remain under pressure until forensic findings and the financial impact are clearly established.

• 🏗️ Strategic Land Addition: Entered joint development agreement for ~18-acre parcel in Thane, Mumbai. 

• 💰 Strong Revenue Potential: Project estimated to generate over ₹7,500 crore revenue. 

• 🏢 Residential Focus: Planned largely as an integrated residential development in a key micro-market. 

• 🚇 Connectivity Tailwinds: Proximity to Thane-Wadala Metro, Borivali twin-tube tunnel and bullet train expected to boost demand. 

• 📍 MMR Expansion Strategy: Marks company’s fourth project in Thane, strengthening presence in high-growth corridor. 

🟢 Impact: Positive
Large revenue potential and expansion in a high-demand micro-market strengthen growth visibility for Godrej Properties.

DEE Development Engineers : Co. Wins ₹173 Cr Order Book Boost

• 🏭 Domestic LOIs: Includes ₹26 crore order from BHEL and ₹42+ crore order from a power sector JV. 

• 🌍 International Orders: Thailand subsidiary bagged US$ 8M + US$ 1.5M contracts from a US-based OEM. 

• ⏳ Execution Timeline: Domestic orders to be delivered within a year; international orders extend up to Dec 2027–2028. 

• 🔗 No Related Party Risk: Company confirmed no promoter interest in awarding entities.

ASHIKA ON IDFC FIRST BANK (ON FRAUD)

View this as an operational risk event, not a deterioration in underlying business fundamentals

The operative phrase in the disclosure is “under reconciliation”, not “loss.”

At this stage, there is insufficient clarity to quantify the eventual P&L impact.

Under a best-case assumption of ~80% recovery, the potential hit could be Rs 120cr.

In a more conservative scenario involving delayed recovery or procedural complexities, the full Rs 590 could be charged upfront

Full loss-estimated 10–15bps impact on RoA and 20–25bps on CET1.

Issue does not relate to core retail asset quality, credit costs, or franchise momentum.

C2C Advance in Focus

India–Brazil; Trade Target & Aviation Boost Announced

• 🌍 Trade Expansion Goal: India and Brazil aim to double bilateral trade to $30 billion by 2030, as per MEA officials.

• ✈️ Embraer Assembly Line: Brazilian President Lula announces that Embraer will set up a regional jet assembly line in India.

• 🛠️ MRO Proposal: PM Modi suggests establishing a dedicated Maintenance, Repair & Overhaul (MRO) facility in India.

• 🤝 Strategic Deepening: Moves signal stronger cooperation across trade, aviation, and manufacturing sectors.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Boosts aviation manufacturing ecosystem, enhances bilateral trade prospects, and supports India’s aerospace ambitions.

Suraj Estate Developers; Approves ₹45 Cr NCD Allotment

• 💼 NCD Issuance: Board approved allotment of 4.5 Cr unlisted, secured, non-convertible debentures (NCDs) of ₹10 face value each.

• 💰 Issue Size: Total fundraising of up to ₹45 Cr via private placement.

• 🏦 Investor & Trustee: Issued to IDBI Trusteeship Services Ltd, acting as Trustee for India Real Estate Investment Fund Series 2.

• ⏳ Tenure & Repayment: 42-month tenure with 24-month principal moratorium; repayment in 18 equal monthly instalments from month 25.

• 📈 Interest Structure: 12% p.a. for first 24 months; 17% p.a. thereafter, payable monthly.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Strengthens liquidity position, though relatively higher post-moratorium interest cost may weigh on finance expenses.

M.K. Exim (India); Promoter Increases Stake via Open Market Purchase

• 🛒 Stake Acquisition: Murli Wadhumal Dialani (Promoter & Whole Time Director) acquired 1,746 equity shares on Feb 19, 2026.

• 💰 Transaction Value: Shares purchased for a total consideration of ₹87,000.

• 📊 Revised Holding: Post acquisition, total holding stands at 37,75,639 equity shares (9.353%) of total equity capital.

• 🏦 Mode of Purchase: Transaction executed through open market purchase on BSE.

🟢 Impact: Positive
Promoter stake increase via open market signals confidence in the company’s prospects.

𝗣𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗴𝗲 𝗚𝗿𝗼𝘂𝗽 𝗣𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗻𝗲𝗿𝘀 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝗕𝗠𝗥𝗖𝗟 𝘁𝗼 𝗔𝗱𝗼𝗽𝘁 𝗕𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗱𝘂𝗿 𝗠𝗲𝘁𝗿𝗼 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 🚇:

Prestige Group has entered into a significant 30-year definitive agreement with Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Limited (BMRCL) to adopt and co-brand the Bellandur Metro Station as "𝗣𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗴𝗲 𝗕𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗱𝘂𝗿 𝗠𝗲𝘁𝗿𝗼 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻." This strategic Public-Private Partnership (PPP) aims to enhance urban infrastructure and multi-modal connectivity in Bengaluru.

Gold Resumes Uptrend Amid Record ETF Buying – Don't Ignore This Signal

Price Surge Regains Momentum  
- MCX Gold back at ₹1.6 lakh per 10 gm; spot gold hits $5,100 internationally.  
- Sharp rebound reflects renewed safe-haven demand amid tariff uncertainty and macro fragmentation.  

Global ETFs Hit Historic Highs  
- World Gold Council: January 2026 saw US$19 bn net inflows – strongest month ever.  
- Combined with 14% gold price rise, global gold ETF AUM reaches record US$669 bn (up 20% m/m).  
- Total holdings approach 4,145 tonnes as investors pile into wealth preservation.  

China Drives ETF Frenzy  
- Chinese gold ETF holdings up 50% in 6 months to 286 tonnes as of Jan 2026.  
- January alone: 38.5 tonnes bought – second-highest ever (after April's 65 tonnes).  
- Retail + institutional rush reflects capital fleeing domestic market stress.  

The Bigger Macro Theme  
- In chaotic, non-linear world: gold = wealth preservation, not just tactical trade.  
- Structural ETF bid + Asian demand provides floor as long as real yields stay suppressed and China exports capital into bullion.  

Key Takeaway: With tariffs dominating headlines, gold's record ETF allocations signal investors prioritizing capital protection over risk assets – a trend likely to persist.

UVS Hospitality & Services Ltd's Board of Directors has approved a significant strategic move: the acquisition of up to 51% of Calcio Restaurants Private Limited. This acquisition will be executed through a share swap, involving the issuance of up to 35,02,671 equity shares of UVS Hospitality at an issue price of ₹100 per share (face value ₹10, premium ₹90), for a total consideration of approximately ₹35.03 Crore.

𝗞𝗲𝘆 𝗛𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗰𝗾𝘂𝗶𝘀𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻:

- 𝗔𝗰𝗾𝘂𝗶𝘀𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗦𝗶𝘇𝗲: Up to 51% of paid-up equity share capital.
- 𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘀𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: Share swap (non-cash) via issuance of UVS Hospitality equity shares.
- 𝗢𝗯𝗷𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲: To consolidate operations, enhance strategic value, and create an integrated corporate structure, leading to synergistic advantages, balance sheet strengthening, improved operational efficiency, and unlocking future growth opportunities.
- 𝗢𝘂𝘁𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗲: Post-acquisition, Calcio Restaurants Private Limited will become a subsidiary of UVS Hospitality.

MOIL Limited has announced a significant victory in its appeal against a GST demand under reverse charge mechanism (RCM) concerning MPGATSVA payments made to the Mining Department of Madhya Pradesh.

𝗞𝗲𝘆 𝗛𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁𝘀:

- 𝗔𝗽𝗽𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗔𝘂𝘁𝗵𝗼𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗥𝘂𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴: The appellate authority has ruled in favor of MOIL Ltd, setting aside the original order that had confirmed a GST demand of ₹20.29 Cr (including an equal penalty).

- 𝗚𝗦𝗧 𝗗𝗲𝗺𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗤𝘂𝗮𝘀𝗵𝗲𝗱: The entire GST demand of ₹20.29 Cr, along with an equivalent penalty of ₹20.29 Cr and estimated interest of ₹15-18 Cr, has been quashed. This effectively eliminates a total risk exposure of approximately ₹55-58 Cr. 💰

- 𝗟𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗴𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝘁𝘂𝘀: The appeal was filed against an Order-in-Original dated 13.12.2024. The Commissioner (Appeals) CGST & Central Excise, Bhopal, vide an Order-in-Appeal dated 29.01.2026, has allowed the appeal.

- 𝗖𝘂𝗿𝗿𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴: As of now, no liability survives. However, this is subject to any further appeals that may be filed by the Department. 🧐

𝗧𝗮𝗸𝗲 𝗦𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝗣𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗻𝗲𝗿𝘀 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝗔𝗻𝘁𝗵𝗿𝗼𝗽𝗶𝗰 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗔𝗜-𝗗𝗿𝗶𝘃𝗲𝗻 𝗛𝗲𝗮𝗹𝘁𝗵𝗰𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗘𝘅𝗽𝗮𝗻𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 🚀:

In aerospace, supply bottlenecks create outsized financial returns. Ultimately, scarcity dictates pricing power. Here’s where the squeeze is happening right now:

1) Engines: Pushing the absolute limits of physics and materials creates natural scarcity. Very few companies can build them, which drives high growth and margins for those who do.

2) Aero-structures: The sheer logistical complexity of building and integrating these frameworks limits the supply pool, heavily rewarding the few who can reliably execute.

3) MRO (Maintenance): Aging fleets, high utilization, and widespread engine hiccups have created a perfect storm of demand, opening big doors for localized repair networks.

Source - Anand Rathi

Brokerages On Infosys AI Day

Jefferies
Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 1880

At its AI Day, Infosys highlighted that IT services will remain key to enterprise AI implementation
Infosys has built its AI platform – Infosys Topaz Fabric – is jointly going to market with AI ecosystem partners
Devised a 2-pronged talent strategy not only to capture new service demand but also to augment its existing service lines
Client AI adoption is increasing with new AI services contributing ~5.5% of revenues in Q3FY26
90% of Infosys's top 200 large clients currently using its AI services

BofA
Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 1840

Several positives on positioning
Net opportunity could be bigger but path is still evolving
Enterprise AI isn’t plug and play
Revenue from AI-first offerings stands at 5.5%, not too different from others that have disclosed the metric
Key risk with AI, as per company, is less about opportunity and more about strategy and execution
Anthropic partnerships signify role of a services company in enterprise AI shift

UBS
Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 1870

Opportunities from AI to outweigh compression
AI potential fast expanding, but organisations not ready
US$300-400bn opportunity from AI to outweigh productivity-led compression
IT Service vendors need to build capabilities via talent, platforms / IP, partnerships and acquisitions
Will keep an eye on Infosys' progress on these fronts going ahead

India’s Core Sector Growth at 4.0% in January 2026 (Provisional)

January 2026 Performance Snapshot

• Core industries expanded 4.0% YoY
• Momentum driven by infrastructure-linked sectors
• Reflects steady demand recovery

What Improved?

• Steel up 9.9%
• Cement up 10.7%
• Electricity up 3.8%
• Coal up 3.1%
• Fertilizer up 3.7%

What Declined?

• Crude Oil down 5.8%
• Natural Gas down 5.0%
• Refinery output unchanged

FY26 Trend (Apr–Jan)

• Cumulative growth at 2.8% (provisional)
• Indicates moderate full-year momentum

Previous Month Update

• December 2025 final growth revised to 4.7%

Investor Takeaway

• Strong steel and cement growth supports infra theme
• Energy production remains weak
• Overall growth steady but not broad-based

Order Details

• Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited receives project sanction orders from Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA)
• Order includes Pump Modules for Liquid Cooling System
• Applicable for LCA Mk2 and 5th Generation AMCA platforms
• Secured additional orders for Compact Heat Exchangers and Fuel Coolers for AMCA

Strategic Significance

• Expands BHEL’s footprint in high-value defence aerospace systems
• Strengthens participation in India’s indigenous fighter jet programs
• Builds on earlier contribution to LCA Tejas variants
• Positions BHEL in next-generation combat aircraft supply chain

Investor Takeaway

• Entry into advanced thermal management systems
• Enhances defence order visibility
• Supports long-term diversification beyond core power business
• Positive signal for defence revenue scaling over coming years

Trump retains tariff options via established trade laws despite the Supreme Court striking down broad IEEPA use.

Court Ruling Impact
The ruling limits unlimited emergency powers under IEEPA but leaves key statutes intact.

Key Alternatives
Section 301 (Trade Act 1974)  
- Targets unfair foreign practices.  
- Requires USTR investigation and hearings.  
- No tariff size cap; previously used against China.

Section 232 (Trade Expansion Act 1962)  
- National security justification.  
- Needs Commerce Department probe (up to 270 days).  
- Applied to steel/aluminum in first term.

Strategic Implications
Both tools demand formal processes but enable sector-specific duties unaffected by the decision.

Key Takeaway  
Sections 301 and 232 ensure tariff continuity through investigations, preserving trade leverage.
Tariffs can also be imposed under Section 301 and Section 232, but they require a detailed investigation and departmental approval.
They can use SECTION 122 with that 10% tariff can applicable for next 150 days

SEBI's ETF Pricing Overhaul: Key Changes Ahead

Current Issue  
- ETF prices tied to assets (stocks, gold etc.).  
- Trading limits use 2-day old values.  
- Sharp market moves cause pricing mismatches.  

SEBI's Proposal  
- Switch to previous day's latest value (1-day old).  
- Ensures ETF reflects recent market shifts.  
- Reduces ETF vs asset value gaps.  
- Delivers fairer investor pricing.  

Gold & Silver ETF Fix  
- Global overnight price changes.  
- Introduce pre-open session.  
- Enables accurate opening prices.  

Investor Benefits  
- Accurate ETF pricing.  
- Reduced confusion.  
- Lower wrong pricing risk.  
- Enhanced transparency, protection.  

Key Takeaway  
From outdated 2-day values to real-time relevant pricing—SEBI boosts ETF fairness and trust.

Selective growth conviction shines in auto/infra/defence/finance; watch execution on margins, US recovery, and debt reduction for FY27 upside.

Indian CDMO

Indian CDMO is facing a cyclical reset, not a structural breakdown. The mix of commercial + clinical molecules naturally creates quarterly volatility, this is a business best judged on annual performance, not QoQ noise.

The 40-70% corrections are largely company-specific, driven by product concentration, molecule delays, or execution gaps. Inventory destocking appears short-term, not demand destruction.

Structurally, the bigger shift remains intact, global sponsors are diversifying supply chains, and India is moving from tactical vendor to strategic partner. However, this is a long-gestation relationship-driven business, not an RFP headline story.

Investor Compass Takeaway:
We are in an investment cycle, not a harvesting cycle. The 5-10 year CDMO opportunity remains intact, but execution quality and diversification will separate the winners.

**AI Infra Details:**  
- Covers multi-GW **datacenters**, **10 GW renewables**, storage, **AI chips**.  
- Starts **120 MW** in **2H26**; scales over **5 years** like global peers.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
- RIL’s massive AI pivot promises superior ROCE, driving value creation to ₹1,803 TP.

**Investment Thesis**

**Key Takeaway**  
Structurally robust T&D tailwinds justify premium—20-25% CAGR conviction intact.

A quick look into the broad market indices!

Sep 2024 was the peak. Markets made a bottom in March 2025. 

Note - Largecap & Midcaps made a new high in January. Smallcaps which fell 25% rallied 18% from the bottom. It is now at a discount of 12%

IC Electricals IPO: A Railway Electrification Play Riding India’s Infra Cycle🚆⚡

Railway engineering company, capability across electrification, traction motors and railway electronics*l

Key Highlights
✅ Operates across high-demand segments like 25 kV OHE electrification, traction substations, alternators and traction motors.

📊 Revenue mix across 3 verticals
- Contracts (45.6%)
- Rotating Machines (33.3%)
- Electronics (21.1%) 

📌 Strong execution track record
- 17 completed projects worth ~₹197 Cr
- 16 ongoing projects worth ~₹181 Cr
- 700+ track km electrification executed.

💰 Financial performance
- FY25 Revenue: ₹121.9 Cr
- EBITDA Margin: 15.2%
- PAT Margin: 7.7%
- ROE: 23.3% 
- ROCE: 17.6%

🔹Core Strengths
- Strong R&D focus keeps products competitive and cost-efficient.
- Modern Haridwar facilities ensure timely execution and operational efficiency.
- Strong after-sales support minimizing downtime and enhancing customer trust.

Mobilise App Lab Limited IPO Opening – A Scalable SaaS Growth Opportunity

AI-enabled ERP solutions driving digital transformation across education, healthcare, supply chain and HR.

Cloud-based, multi-tenant platforms with product-led revenue model.

IPO opens 23rd Feb 2026 | Price Band ₹75–₹80 | Issue Size ₹20.09 Cr

Mobilise App Lab Limited IPO: Riding India’s AI-Driven SaaS Growth Wave

AI-powered enterprise solutions.

IPO opens 23rd Feb 2026 – Positioned for digital-first growth.

**Bernstein: India Payments Sector Outlook**

**Revenue Growth**  
- Net revenue pool ~$150 Bn; expands to ~$385 Bn by FY30E.  
- "Zero-margin UPI" overstated; monetisation accelerating.

**Key Drivers**  
- Merchant payments ~75% of revenues, profitability engine.  
- Online payments, device monetisation offset MDR drag.  
- Cashless volumes ~20% CAGR next 5 years.  
- Transaction credit: High-monetisation upside.  
- Consolidation boosts scaled players' profitability.

**Stock Calls**  
- Paytm: Underperform (TP ₹1,600).  
- SBI Cards: Outperform (TP ₹780).

**Key Takeaway**: Structural tailwinds favor leaders—bet on credit/monetisation plays over UPI pure-plays.

**Nuvama: Realty Sector Q3 Review**

**Booking & Sales**  
- Top 21 players: Volumes +15% YoY, realisations +5% YoY.  
- Collections robust: +21% YoY Q3, +15% overall.

**Launches & Affordability**  
- Uneven launches: Top 16 down 18% YoY in Q3.  
- Affordability caps price hikes; shift to volume focus.  
- QoQ volume moderation due to execution timing.

**Market Dynamics**  
- Top-11 listed players hold steady 15% share.  
- Healthy Q4FY26 launch pipeline to drive bookings.

**Stock Drivers**  
- Performance tied to product mix, regional exposure, execution.

Carlton swings from VIP to Safari!

In an interesting development on the Indian bourses, Safari Industries has announced a 20 year licensing agreement with Carlton Retail to exclusively use the “Carlton” brand in India for luggage and accessories.

This comes after the long running trademark dispute between VIP Industries and Carlton Shoes over ownership of the Carlton name in India. The court had barred VIP from using the brand for its luggage line in the domestic market. Safari is now set to retail backpacks and luggage under the Carlton brand.

For context, Carlton contributed about 4 to 6% of VIP’s FY25 turnover, translating to roughly ₹130 cr.

Key agreement details
* Initial term of 20 years with an option to renew for another 20 years at Safari’s discretion
* License fee of ₹45 lakh annually or 5% of net sales, whichever is higher
* Interest free refundable security deposit of ₹100 cr to be paid to Carlton Retail in tranches

Why this could matter for Safari?
1️⃣ Firmer presence in the premium segment through an established global brand. Carlton can complement Safari Select & Urban Jungle & help Safari compete more effectively with players like Samsonite, Mokobara & Delsey.
2️⃣ Safari’s premium portfolio currently contributes only about 6% of revenue. Scaling Carlton could accelerate premium mix improvement and support margins, given the higher price realisations in this segment.

Execution and brand positioning will be the key monitorables from here.

In 2004, Carlton partnered VIP when Sudhir Jatia was part of the company. In a full circle development, Carlton has now entered into a new agreement with Safari Industries India Ltd. which Jatia currently owns.
